checks.a_analysis: All text elements in the output JSON have a direct basis in the source document. No invented section headings, body text, or conclusions were found.
There are no phone numbers, emails, or URLs in the source. Names of individuals (Edward Lucas, Taryn Madden) and dates (September 4 and 5, 2024) were verified and found to match the source perfectly.
Both pages are represented in the output JSON. All major sections (Header, Amendment Details, Period of Performance, Amendment Execution, Signatures) are present.

The 'Contract Award' table in the source document contains 32 project rows on page 1 and 1 additional project row on page 2 (APP-01987), for a total of 33 project data entries. The output JSON table only includes 3 project rows (1, 5, and APP-01987), omitting approximately 30 rows (91% of data rows). This is a critical loss of tabular data.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on Page 1 contains 32 project rows (Project #1 through #32). Table continuation on Page 2 contains 1 project row (APP-01987).
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON table contains only 3 data rows for projects: '1', '5', and 'Replaced APP-09700 above' (APP-01987).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The 'Amendment Details' section is intended to specify the new contract award details through the provided table. Since the table has lost over 90% of its content, the section fails to convey the substantial majority of the information found in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains 33 specific project award entries with detailed cost data.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output contains only 3 project award entries, losing the vast majority of the amendment's core technical content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output slightly simplifies the paragraph regarding removals by omitting visible punctuation and parenthetical text (e.g., '(658...)'), but the primary meaning remains intact and falls within acceptable OCR limits when considering the major failures elsewhere.

checks.a_analysis: No content duplication was observed. Each text block and table row present in the output appears only once, corresponding to its unique occurrence in the source document.

The heading hierarchy is flawed as it skips H1 and H2 levels entirely. All headings, including the document title and major section headers, are assigned at the H3 level. This creates a flat structure that does not represent the logical hierarchy of the source document, where 'AMENDMENT NUMBER THREE (3)' is clearly the primary title and 'PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE:' is a major section heading.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document contains a clear title 'AMENDMENT NUMBER THREE (3)' and major sections such as 'PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE:'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output uses H3 for the title (element index heading-1) and for major sections (element index heading-5 and heading-6).
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document headers and section titles.

checks.a_analysis: The main contract award table is severely truncated. While the source table spans two pages and includes 32 numbered rows plus multiple summary and replacement rows, the output table only contains 5 data/summary rows in total, skipping rows 2-4 and 6-32. This represents a significant failure in capturing the table's structural organization and complexity.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table on page 1 contains rows numbered 1 through 32. Page 2 contains additional replacement and summary rows for the same table.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table in the JSON contains only 5 rows: Project 1, Project 5, Replaced APP-09700 above, Total Amended Contract Amount, and the Amendment #3 summary row.
checks.b_evidence_location: Amendment Details section, 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order of the elements included in the output follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document.
There are no formal lists (bulleted or numbered) in the source document to evaluate; all numbered items belong to the table structure.
There are no hyperlinks in the source document, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully replicates the visual organization of the source PDF. It uses a single-column layout suitable for web viewing while maintaining the logical order of sections: header information, amendment details, the award table, performance period, and signature blocks. The inclusion of a sidebar 'Contents' menu is a standard accessible enhancement and does not disrupt the visual flow of information.

The main data table, titled 'Contract Award replaced with the below table' in the PDF, is severely truncated in the HTML rendering. In the source PDF, this table contains 32 distinct project rows spanning across pages 1 and 2. However, the HTML screenshot shows only three data rows (Project 1, Project 5, and the replaced project row for APP-01987). This constitutes a major loss of data and a failure to render the table correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: The table starting on page 1 and ending on page 2 contains 32 numbered project rows (1-32) plus several replacement rows.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table in the HTML only renders rows for Project 1, Project 5, and the bottom replacement row for APP-01987, skipping the vast majority of the data.
checks.b_evidence_location: Amendment Details section, 'Replaced Contract Award Details' table

checks.a_analysis: The NC Department of Public Safety (DPS) logo, which is the primary meaningful image in the source document, is correctly included and rendered in the HTML document header.

There is no evidence of visual duplication of body content (paragraphs, specific table data, or signatures). The presence of headings in both the sidebar navigation and the main body is an intentional accessibility feature and not a defect.
